title: Blackstone Ribeye with Beef Tallow
description: Fast griddle-seared ribeye with beef tallow.
steps: Rest at room temperature while the griddle heats.
notes: Use the thermometer for consistency. Rest before slicing.
recommendedReason: The Blackstone gives the best crust and enough room to manage hot and cooler zones.

oven.note: Good for reverse sear, but it needs a hot finish for crust.
instructions: Cook steak on a rack until nearly done.
Finish very hot with beef tallow.

airFryer.note: Works for convenience, but crust is weaker than griddle searing.
instructions: Preheat COSORI air fryer to 200 C.
Cook steak for 7 to 10 minutes, turning once.

blackstone.note: Best surface contact and crust.
instructions: Melt beef tallow on the hot zone.
Sear steak for 3 to 5 minutes per side.
Move to a cooler zone if the outside browns before the center is ready.

title: Crispy Beef Patties
description: Thin beef patties with crisp browned edges and simple seasoning.
steps: Divide the beef into loose balls.
Keep the meat loose so the patties brown instead of steaming.
notes: Best served hot from the griddle. Use higher-fat mince for better browning.
recommendedReason: The Blackstone gives the best crust and enough surface area for multiple patties.

oven.note: Works for batch cooking, but crust is weaker.
instructions: Press patties thin on a lined tray.
Bake for 10 to 14 minutes, turning once.

airFryer.note: Good for a small clean batch, but less crust than Blackstone.
instructions: Preheat COSORI air fryer to 200 C.
Place patties in a single layer.
Cook for 8 to 10 minutes, turning once.

blackstone.note: Best browning and fat rendering.
instructions: Melt beef tallow on the surface.
Smash beef balls thin with a firm spatula.
Cook until the edges brown deeply, then flip once.

title: Soft Scrambled Eggs with Butter
description: Soft scrambled eggs cooked gently with butter.
reason_not_strict: Includes butter, which is dairy.
steps: Use a cooler zone for soft eggs.
notes: Remove the eggs while still glossy.
recommendedReason: A cooler Blackstone zone works well when cooking eggs alongside other food.

oven.note: Oven heat is too broad for soft scrambled eggs.

airFryer.note: Air fryer airflow sets eggs unevenly.

blackstone.note: Best among the listed appliances if using a cool zone.
instructions: Set one Blackstone zone to low.

title: Pork Belly Bites
description: Salted pork belly cubes with crisp edges.
steps: Cut pork belly into 3 cm cubes.
notes: Smaller cubes crisp faster.
recommendedReason: The COSORI air fryer renders fat efficiently and crisps edges without much handling.

oven.note: Best for a larger batch.
instructions: Spread pork belly on a rack over a tray.
Roast for 25 to 35 minutes, turning once.

airFryer.note: Best crisping for a small batch.
instructions: Preheat COSORI air fryer to 190 C.
Cook in a single layer for 25 to 30 minutes.

blackstone.note: Works, but fat splatter is more active.
instructions: Cook cubes on a cooler zone first to render fat.
Move to hotter zone to crisp all sides.

title: Salmon in Butter
description: Salmon cooked with butter and salt.
reason_not_strict: Includes butter, which is dairy.
steps: Keep the skin side dry if using skin-on salmon.
notes: Keep heat moderate so butter browns without burning.
recommendedReason: The air fryer cooks salmon quickly and evenly with minimal handling.

oven.note: Good for multiple fillets.
instructions: Place salmon in a small dish with butter on top.

airFryer.note: Best for speed and clean handling.
instructions: Preheat COSORI air fryer to 180 C.
Top with butter for the final 2 minutes.

blackstone.note: Works if the surface is clean and lightly greased.
instructions: Flip briefly and finish with butter.

title: Carnivore Flatbread
description: Flexible egg, cream cheese, and pork rind flatbread.
reason_not_strict: Uses dairy and a bread-style format for convenience.
steps: Blend eggs, cream cheese, pork rind crumbs, and salt until smooth.
Let the batter settle for 2 minutes.
notes: Let it cool before cutting so it holds together.
recommendedReason: The oven gives the most even set across a thin sheet.

oven.note: Best even texture.
instructions: Bake on parchment for 18 to 22 minutes.

airFryer.note: Works only in smaller pieces because of basket size.
instructions: Preheat COSORI air fryer to 175 C.
Cook small parchment-supported portions for 9 to 12 minutes.

blackstone.note: The batter is too loose for a griddle until it sets.

title: Cottage Cheese Flatbread
description: Soft dairy-rich flatbread based on cottage cheese and eggs.
reason_not_strict: Uses cottage cheese and a bread-style format.
steps: Blend cottage cheese, eggs, parmesan, and salt until smooth.
Spread on parchment in a thin rectangle.
notes: This is intentionally dairy-heavy.
recommendedReason: The oven dries and sets the dairy batter evenly.

oven.note: Best structure.
instructions: Bake for 30 to 40 minutes until set and browned at the edges.

airFryer.note: Good for small portions.
instructions: Preheat COSORI air fryer to 160 C.
Cook small portions on parchment for 18 to 22 minutes.

blackstone.note: The wet dairy batter does not set cleanly on the griddle.

title: 2-Ingredient Carnivore Flatbread
description: Simple flatbread made from eggs and cottage cheese or cream cheese.
reason_not_strict: Bread-style format for convenience and may use dairy.

ingredients.original: 120 g cottage cheese or cream cheese

steps: Blend eggs with cottage cheese or cream cheese.
notes: Parmesan is optional but improves structure.
recommendedReason: The oven gives the most reliable flat, even result.

oven.note: Most reliable.
instructions: Bake for 25 to 35 minutes until set.

airFryer.note: Good for one smaller flatbread.
instructions: Preheat COSORI air fryer to 160 C.
Cook on parchment for 12 to 15 minutes.

blackstone.note: Use a very thin layer and low heat.
instructions: Spread a thin layer on a lightly greased cooler zone.
Cook until set, then flip carefully.

title: 3-Ingredient Carnivore-Style Burger Buns
description: Small carnivore-style buns made with eggs, cream cheese, and pork rind crumbs.
reason_not_strict: Uses dairy and a bun-style format.
steps: Divide into a greased muffin-top pan or silicone moulds.
notes: Toast cut sides in beef tallow for more structure.
recommendedReason: The oven gives the buns time to set without burning the outside.

oven.note: Best structure.

airFryer.note: Works in small silicone moulds.
instructions: Preheat COSORI air fryer to 165 C.
Cook in small moulds for 10 to 14 minutes.

blackstone.note: The batter needs mould support and even surrounding heat.

title: Air Fryer Meatballs
description: Simple beef meatballs cooked quickly in the COSORI air fryer.
reason_not_strict: Includes parmesan, which is dairy.
steps: Mix beef, egg, parmesan, and salt.
notes: Do not pack the meat too tightly.
recommendedReason: The COSORI air fryer browns the outside quickly while keeping the batch contained.

oven.note: Good for larger batches.
instructions: Bake meatballs on a tray for 14 to 18 minutes.

airFryer.note: Best browning for this size batch.
instructions: Preheat COSORI air fryer to 190 C.
Cook meatballs in a single layer for 10 to 12 minutes.
Use reheat at 180 C for 3 to 4 minutes for leftovers.

blackstone.note: Works if flattened slightly; round meatballs roll.
instructions: Cook on all sides until browned and cooked through.

title: Carnivore Vanilla Ice Cream
description: Cream and egg yolk ice cream with vanilla.
reason_not_strict: Includes heavy dairy and vanilla.
steps: Temper egg yolks with warm cream.
Cook over low heat until lightly thickened.
notes: This is unsweetened. Keep heat gentle to avoid scrambling the yolks.

recommendedMethod.label: Stovetop custard + ice cream maker
recommendedMethod.reason: This recipe needs gentle custard cooking and churning. Oven, air fryer, and Blackstone are not suitable.
instructions: Churn in an ice cream maker.

oven.note: Oven heat is not suitable for churning ice cream.

airFryer.note: Air fryer heat is not suitable for custard or ice cream.

blackstone.note: A griddle is not suitable for custard or ice cream.

title: Egg Yolk Custard
description: Small custard made with egg yolks, cream, and butter.
reason_not_strict: Includes cream and butter, which are dairy.
notes: Low heat is the main control point.

recommendedMethod.label: Gentle stovetop custard
recommendedMethod.reason: Direct stovetop control is better than the three listed appliances for this custard.
instructions: Cook over low heat while whisking.
Remove from heat and whisk in butter.

oven.note: Works as a water bath custard if you prefer oven control.
instructions: Place ramekins in a tray with hot water.

airFryer.note: Airflow and direct heat can scramble the custard.

blackstone.note: Griddle heat is too direct for custard.

title: Mascarpone Cream Bowl
description: Quick cold dairy bowl with mascarpone and cream.
reason_not_strict: Includes heavy dairy.
steps: Chill briefly if you want a firmer texture.
notes: No cooking needed.

recommendedMethod.label: No-cook chilled preparation
recommendedMethod.reason: This dessert is best mixed cold and chilled. Oven, air fryer, and Blackstone do not apply.
instructions: Whisk mascarpone, cream, and salt until smooth.

oven.note: Oven heat does not apply to this no-cook dessert.

airFryer.note: Air fryer heat does not apply to this no-cook dessert.

blackstone.note: A griddle does not apply to this no-cook dessert.

title: Air Fryer Beef Meatballs
description: Simple beef meatballs for the COSORI air fryer.
steps: Mix beef, egg, and salt gently.
Chill for 5 minutes if the mixture feels loose.
notes: Do not pack the meat too tightly or the texture becomes dense.
recommendedReason: The air fryer browns the outside while keeping the batch contained and easy to clean.

oven.note: Good for larger batches.
instructions: Place meatballs on a lined tray.
Bake for 14 to 18 minutes until done.

airFryer.note: Best balance of browning and convenience.
instructions: Preheat COSORI air fryer to 190 C.
Cook meatballs in a single layer for 10 to 12 minutes.
Shake or turn once if needed.

blackstone.note: Works if flattened slightly. Round meatballs can roll.
instructions: Cook on several sides until browned and done.
Move to a cooler zone if browning too fast.

title: Beef and Pork Parmesan Meatballs
description: Tender mixed meatballs with parmesan for a dairy-friendly version.
reason_not_strict: Includes parmesan, which is dairy.
steps: Mix beef, pork, parmesan, egg, and salt.
Rest for 5 minutes before cooking so they hold shape.
notes: Parmesan adds salt, so season carefully.
recommendedReason: The air fryer gives fast browning and keeps the cheese from making a mess on a flat surface.

oven.note: Good for a full tray.
instructions: Bake meatballs on a lined tray for 16 to 20 minutes.

airFryer.note: Best for browning and easy cleanup.
instructions: Preheat COSORI air fryer to 190 C.
Cook in a single layer for 11 to 14 minutes.

blackstone.note: Works if shaped as small patties instead of balls.
instructions: Cook until browned on both sides and done in the center.

title: Carnivore Smash Burger Bowl
description: Smash burger pieces served with egg, bacon, and cheese in a bowl.
reason_not_strict: Includes cheese and optional dairy toppings.
steps: Prepare bowls before cooking so everything can be served hot.
notes: Good when you want burger flavour without buns.
recommendedReason: The Blackstone can cook beef, bacon, and eggs in separate zones at the same time.

oven.note: Works as a tray bake, but the burger crust is weaker.
instructions: Bake bacon and flattened beef portions on a tray.
Cook eggs separately or bake in a small dish.

airFryer.note: Works in stages, but capacity is limited.
instructions: Cook bacon in the air fryer at 190 C until crisp.
Cook beef patties at 200 C for 8 to 10 minutes.
Top with cheese and serve with cooked eggs.

blackstone.note: Best for batch cooking and crust.
instructions: Cook bacon pieces until crisp and move to a cooler area.
Smash beef balls thin and brown deeply.
Cook eggs on a cooler zone and assemble bowls.

title: Minced Beef and Egg Skillet
description: Fast beef mince and eggs for a simple filling meal.
steps: Keep eggs ready before the beef finishes.
notes: Cook the eggs gently so they stay soft.
recommendedReason: The Blackstone gives enough space to brown the beef and finish the eggs gently on a cooler zone.

oven.note: Oven heat is too broad for soft eggs and loose mince.

airFryer.note: Works, but texture is drier.
instructions: Cook beef in an air fryer-safe dish at 190 C for 7 to 9 minutes, stirring once.
Add whisked eggs and cook 3 to 5 minutes more until just set.

blackstone.note: Best for speed and control.
instructions: Brown the beef and salt it.
Move beef aside and lower one zone.
Add eggs and stir gently into the beef.

title: Oven Carnivore Meatloaf
description: Simple meatloaf made from beef, pork, egg, and parmesan.
reason_not_strict: Includes parmesan, which is dairy.
steps: Shape into a loaf or press into a loaf tin.
notes: Good for leftovers. Slice after resting so it holds together.
recommendedReason: The oven cooks the loaf evenly and keeps the shape stable.

oven.note: Best structure and even cooking.
instructions: Bake the loaf for 40 to 50 minutes until done.

airFryer.note: Works only as smaller mini loaves.
instructions: Shape into small loaves that fit the basket.
Cook at 180 C for 18 to 25 minutes.

blackstone.note: A full meatloaf is too thick for a griddle. Use patties instead.

title: Cheese-Stuffed Beef Patties
description: Beef patties filled with melting cheese.
reason_not_strict: Includes cheese, which is dairy.
steps: Place cheese in the center of 3 rounds.
Cover with remaining rounds and seal edges well.
notes: Seal the edges carefully or cheese will leak.
recommendedReason: The Blackstone gives good crust while letting you control leaking cheese quickly.

oven.note: Good for less handling.
instructions: Bake stuffed patties on a lined tray for 14 to 18 minutes.

airFryer.note: Works, but leaking cheese can stick.
instructions: Preheat COSORI air fryer to 190 C.
Cook patties for 9 to 12 minutes in a single layer.

blackstone.note: Best crust and control.
instructions: Cook patties until browned on the first side.
Move to cooler zone if cheese starts leaking.

title: Beef Mince Breakfast Scramble
description: Ground beef, eggs, butter, and cheese for a fast breakfast.
reason_not_strict: Includes butter and cheese, which are dairy.
notes: Keep the heat moderate after adding eggs.
recommendedReason: The Blackstone handles the beef and eggs quickly with separate heat zones.

oven.note: Oven cooking makes the scramble dry.

airFryer.note: Works in a dish but gives less control.
instructions: Cook beef in an air fryer-safe dish at 190 C for 6 to 8 minutes, stirring once.
Cook 3 to 5 minutes more until set.

blackstone.note: Best for speed and texture.
instructions: Lower one zone and melt butter.
Fold in cheese and serve when just set.

title: Air Fryer Kofta-Style Beef Logs
description: Beef mince shaped into logs for fast air fryer cooking.
steps: Brush or rub lightly with beef tallow.
notes: Make the logs similar thickness so they cook evenly.
recommendedReason: The air fryer cooks the logs evenly with minimal handling.

oven.note: Good for a larger batch.
instructions: Place logs on a lined tray.
Bake for 12 to 16 minutes, turning once.

airFryer.note: Best convenience and shape retention.
instructions: Preheat COSORI air fryer to 190 C.
Cook logs in a single layer for 9 to 12 minutes.

blackstone.note: Good crust if handled carefully.
instructions: Cook logs on all sides until browned.
Move to a cooler zone if browning too quickly.

title: Pork and Beef Burger Bites
description: Small beef and pork bites for easy portions and leftovers.
notes: Useful for children because portions are small and easy to reheat.
recommendedReason: The air fryer cooks small bites quickly and makes reheating easy.

oven.note: Good for batch cooking.
instructions: Bake bites on a lined tray for 12 to 16 minutes.

airFryer.note: Best for small bites.
instructions: Preheat COSORI air fryer to 190 C.
Cook in a single layer for 8 to 11 minutes.

blackstone.note: Good if you want stronger crust.
instructions: Flip once when the first side is browned.

title: Carnivore Mince Pizza Base
description: A thin beef mince base topped with cheese.
reason_not_strict: Uses a pizza-style format and cheese.
steps: Mix beef, egg, parmesan, and salt.
notes: Keep the base thin. A thick base behaves more like meatloaf.
recommendedReason: The oven sets the mince base evenly and melts the cheese without needing to flip it.

oven.note: Best structure.
instructions: Bake the mince base for 12 to 15 minutes.
Add mozzarella and return to oven for 5 to 8 minutes.

airFryer.note: Basket size and airflow make a full pizza base awkward.

blackstone.note: Works as smaller griddle rounds, not as one full base.
instructions: Cook small thin mince rounds until set.
Add cheese and cover briefly to melt.

title: Creamy Beef Mince Bowl
description: Ground beef finished with cream cheese and butter.
reason_not_strict: Includes cream cheese and butter, which are dairy.
steps: Keep dairy ready so it can melt into the hot beef.
Use low heat after adding dairy.
notes: This is best finished in a pan because dairy can split over high heat.

recommendedMethod.label: Stovetop pan
recommendedMethod.reason: A pan gives better control when melting cream cheese and cream into browned beef.
instructions: Brown beef in a pan over medium-high heat.
Add butter, cream cheese, and cream.
Stir until creamy and serve hot.

oven.note: Can be turned into a baked bowl, but it is less creamy.
instructions: Mix with dairy in a small dish.
Bake until hot and lightly browned on top.

airFryer.note: Air fryer heat can dry the beef and split the dairy.

blackstone.note: Good for browning the beef, but finish the cream sauce in a pan.
instructions: Move beef to a pan or bowl.
Stir in butter, cream cheese, and cream while hot.

title: Chocolate-Style Carnivore Ice Cream
description: Creamy chocolate-style ice cream using cocoa as a practical flavour option.
reason_not_strict: Includes dairy, vanilla, and cocoa, so it is not strict carnivore.
steps: Whisk cocoa into warm cream until smooth.
Temper egg yolks with the warm cream.
notes: Use only if cocoa fits your version of the cookbook.

recommendedMethod.reason: The cocoa needs to dissolve into a custard base before churning.
instructions: Warm cream with cocoa and salt.

oven.note: Oven heat is not suitable for churning ice cream.

airFryer.note: Air fryer heat is not suitable for custard or ice cream.

blackstone.note: A griddle is not suitable for custard or ice cream.

reason_not_strict: Includes dairy, coffee, and optional vanilla.
steps: Temper yolks with the warm liquid.
notes: Reduce the coffee first if you want a stronger flavour without adding too much liquid.

recommendedMethod.label: Stovetop custard + ice cream maker
recommendedMethod.reason: Coffee ice cream needs a chilled custard base and churning.

oven.note: Oven heat is not suitable for churning ice cream.

airFryer.note: Air fryer heat is not suitable for custard or ice cream.

blackstone.note: A griddle is not suitable for custard or ice cream.

title: Mascarpone Ice Cream
description: Dense dairy-rich ice cream with mascarpone and cream.
reason_not_strict: Includes heavy dairy.

notes: A no-cook version is richer and denser. Use fresh eggs you are comfortable using.

recommendedMethod.label: No-cook chilled mix + ice cream maker
recommendedMethod.reason: Mascarpone works well as a cold mix and does not need oven, air fryer, or Blackstone cooking.
instructions: Churn in an ice cream maker.

airFryer.note: Air fryer heat is not suitable for this cold churned dessert.

blackstone.note: A griddle is not suitable for this cold churned dessert.

description: Creamy ice cream with butter and egg yolk custard.
reason_not_strict: Includes heavy dairy.
steps: Melt butter gently into the cream.
Temper egg yolks with the warm cream.
notes: Use moderate heat so the butter emulsifies instead of separating.

recommendedMethod.reason: The butter needs to emulsify into a gentle custard before churning.

oven.note: Oven heat is not suitable for churning ice cream.

airFryer.note: Air fryer heat is not suitable for custard or ice cream.

blackstone.note: A griddle is not suitable for custard or ice cream.

notes: Use only if Greek yogurt fits your version of the cookbook.

recommendedMethod.reason: This dessert is mixed cold and frozen, not cooked with the listed appliances.

title: Air Fryer Egg Bites with Cheese

reason_not_strict: Includes cheese and cream, which are dairy.

Cook thin portions on parchment for 5 to 8 minutes.

title: Air Fryer Chicken Thighs

notes: Cook skin-side up for better crisping.

Roast for 30 to 40 minutes until done.

Cook for 20 to 24 minutes until done.

blackstone.note: Works, but skin can stick if moved too early.

title: Oven Bacon-Wrapped Chicken
description: Chicken breast wrapped in bacon for an easy oven meal.
steps: Add small pieces of butter if desired.
notes: Do not oversalt if the bacon is already salty.
recommendedReason: The oven cooks the wrapped chicken evenly and helps the bacon render around it.

oven.note: Best even cooking.
instructions: Bake on a lined tray for 22 to 30 minutes until done.

airFryer.note: Good for smaller pieces.

Cook in a single layer for 15 to 20 minutes.

blackstone.note: Works but bacon can unwrap during turning.

title: Pork Chops with Butter
description: Simple pork chops cooked with butter or tallow.
reason_not_strict: Includes butter if used.
notes: Rest before cutting.
recommendedReason: The Blackstone gives the best surface browning and fast control.

oven.note: Good for hands-off cooking.
instructions: Bake chops on a tray for 14 to 20 minutes until done.

airFryer.note: Good for small batches.

Cook chops for 10 to 14 minutes, turning once.

title: Brown Butter Topping
description: Nutty browned butter for steak, eggs, fish, or patties.
reason_not_strict: Includes butter, which is dairy.
steps: Use a light-coloured pan if possible so you can see browning.
notes: Remove from heat once it smells nutty and the solids are golden.

recommendedMethod.label: Stovetop saucepan
recommendedMethod.reason: Brown butter needs controlled direct pan heat, not oven, air fryer, or Blackstone cooking.
instructions: Stir or swirl until milk solids turn golden.
Remove from heat and salt lightly.

oven.note: Oven browning is harder to control for a small amount of butter.

airFryer.note: Air fryer heat is not suitable for controlled butter browning.

blackstone.note: Blackstone surface heat is too broad for controlled butter browning unless using a separate pan.

title: Parmesan Crust Topping
description: Simple parmesan crust for patties, chicken, or buns.
reason_not_strict: Includes parmesan, which is dairy.
steps: Use butter to help the topping stick if needed.
notes: Use sparingly because parmesan is salty.
recommendedReason: The oven melts and browns the topping evenly.

oven.note: Best even browning.
instructions: Sprinkle topping over cooked meat or buns.
Bake at 200 C for 5 to 8 minutes until melted and browned.

airFryer.note: Good for small portions.
instructions: Air fry at 190 C for 3 to 5 minutes until browned.

blackstone.note: Works if sprinkled onto hot patties, but loose topping can scatter.
instructions: Add topping directly onto hot cooked patties.
Serve once the cheese sets slightly.

title: Cream Cheese Sauce
description: Simple creamy sauce for mince, meatballs, eggs, or chicken.
reason_not_strict: Includes cream cheese, cream, and butter.
steps: Whisk gradually for a smooth sauce.
notes: Do not boil hard or the sauce may split.

recommendedMethod.label: Stovetop saucepan
recommendedMethod.reason: A saucepan gives the low controlled heat needed for a smooth dairy sauce.

oven.note: Oven heat can split the sauce unless it is baked into another dish.

airFryer.note: Air fryer heat is not suitable for a loose dairy sauce.

blackstone.note: Blackstone can brown meat, but the sauce should be finished in a pan.

title: Beef Tallow Cooking Base
description: Rendered beef tallow for cooking patties, steaks, eggs, and pork.
steps: Keep heat low so the fat renders slowly.
notes: Store only after fully cooled and strained. Use normal food storage judgment.

recommendedMethod.label: Stovetop rendering
recommendedMethod.reason: Slow controlled rendering is best done in a saucepan, not on the three listed appliances.
instructions: Render fat gently over low heat.
Strain when the pieces are browned and fat is clear.

oven.note: Oven rendering works but takes longer and needs a suitable dish.
instructions: Place chopped fat in a covered oven-safe dish.

airFryer.note: Air fryer heat and airflow are not practical for rendering fat.

blackstone.note: A flat griddle is not suitable for slow fat rendering.
